message:
feat(approval): Cx-first issue (allow-once approval) (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Begin Cx-first migration of approval.rs. Previously 0 of 10 async
methods on `ApprovalStore` had `_with_cx` siblings.

Addition (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `ApprovalStore::issue_with_cx(cx, input, summary)` — Cx-first
    parallel of `issue`. Threads cx through the two storage calls
    (count_active_approvals + insert_approval_token) via
    checkpoints at two seams:
      - pre-flight: pre-cancelled cx returns before the count
        query.
      - between count and insert: a cancel during/after the count
        skips the token insert.

Cancellation-safety contract: the token is inserted into storage
AFTER BOTH checkpoints, so a cancel during any checkpoint window
leaves no token on disk — no partial state. A cancel during the
insert itself is out of scope (StorageHandle has no `_with_cx`
surface yet — 147 async fns without cx).

Error mapping: cancellation errors are wrapped in
`Error::Runtime(String)` (the ad-hoc variant noted in the
frankenterm-core error conventions). The `max_active_tokens`
limit check continues to use `Error::Policy` per the legacy path,
so existing callers see the same semantic error classification.

Regression test (new)
`issue_with_cx_matches_legacy_and_is_consumable`:

  * Sets up a StorageHandle + registers a pane (same harness as
    the legacy `issue_and_consume_allow_once` test).
  * Calls `store.issue_with_cx(&cx, &input, None)`.
  * Asserts structural fields on the returned ApprovalRequest:
      - `allow_once_full_hash.starts_with("sha256:")` (token hash
        format preserved).
      - `command == format!("ft approve {allow_once_code}")`
        (CLI command format preserved).
  * Asserts the Cx-first-issued token is consumable by the
    legacy `consume` path — proves the token was written to
    storage with the same schema/fingerprint the legacy consumer
    expects. Regression guard: if a future refactor of
    `issue_with_cx` wrote a different ApprovalTokenRecord shape,
    the legacy consume would fail to match and the test would
    surface that.
  * Asserts double-consume returns None (same exhaustion
    invariant as legacy).

Cx-first approval.rs progress:
  * `issue`                          ✓ (tick 59, this commit)
  * `issue_for_plan`                 — pending
  * `consume`                        — pending
  * `consume_with_context`           — pending
  * `consume_for_plan`               — pending
  * `consume_for_plan_with_context`  — pending
  * `attach_to_decision`             — pending (pure wrapper)

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * approval:: — 95 passed / 0 failed (tick 58: 94 -> tick 59: 95,
    +1 new parity test)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (34.40s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
